title: symlinked device paths not supported as "valid device nodes"
body:
[//]: **kind bug**

**Description**

when attempting to use `/dev/serial/by-id` to attach devices to containers, `podman` complains they are `not a valid device`.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman run -it --rm -v /opt/zigbeemqtt-data:/app/data:Z --device /dev/serial/by-id/usb-Texas_Instruments_TI_CC2531_USB_CDC___0X00124B0018ED293B-if00:/dev/ttyACM0:rwm -u 10101 koenkk/zigbee2mqtt`

**Describe the results you received:**
`/dev/serial/by-id/usb-Texas_Instruments_TI_CC2531_USB_CDC___0X00124B0018ED293B-if00 is not a valid device: not a device node`

**Describe the results you expected:**
running container

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Not sure if this is a valid bug or not, but I don't see why it shouldn't work.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
1d [root:/opt/zigbeemqtt-data] # podman version
Version:       0.11.2-dev
Go Version:    go1.10.2
Git Commit:    "7f866bb2a3ccca733c98cf70e621681ecbb8fa06"
Built:         Mon Nov 19 14:44:40 2018
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```

    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 7237005312

  cpus: 12

  kernel: 3.10.0-862.14.4.el7.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 304h 57m 40.75s (Approximately 12.67 days)
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
CentOS7 host

comments.body:
This doesn't seem to work for any type of symlinked device at all:

```
lrwxrwxrwx.  1 root root           7 Dec 28 10:29 zwave0 -> ttyACM0
```

```
/bin/podman run -d -v /opt/homeassistant-configs:/opt/homeassistant:Z --net host --name hass \
  --device /dev/zwave0:/dev/zwave0:rwm -u 10101 --env-file /opt/hass.env \
  quay.io/thoraxe/home-assistant:0.84.6-1
/dev/zwave0 is not a valid device: not a device node
```
Ah, symlinks would explain it - I don't think we attempt to resolve links and just treat the given path as a device node.
